title: Delicate Flaky Syrian Wagyu Flatbread
description: A salty and flaky Syrian flatbread dish featuring Wagyu beef and a mirepoix base, perfect for a light and raw-friendly meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, salt, black pepper, and sumac.
Gradually add the olive oil and water to the bowl, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a few slices of Wagyu beef in the center of each circle, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Top the beef with a spoonful of mirepoix and fold the edges of the dough up over the filling to form a flatbread.
Brush the tops of the flatbreads with a little olive oil and bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
